data.title: CVS Health, Cigna sue Arkansas over law banning PBM-owned pharmacies
data.description:
CVS Health (NYSE:CVS) and Cigna (NYSE:CI), which operate some of the largest pharmacy benefit managers (PBMs) in the U.S., have sued Arkansas, seeking to block a state law that is set to ban PBMs from owning or operating pharmacies in the state.

CVS Health (NYSE:CVS) and Cigna’s (NYSE:CI) PBM unit, Express Scripts, filed separate lawsuits in Little Rock federal court on Thursday in a bid to obtain a declaration that the law passed in April was unconstitutional and an order barring its implementation.

The executive director and members of the state’s pharmacy regulator, the Arkansas State Board of Pharmacy, have been named as defendants in the lawsuits, which have drawn the ire of Arkansas Gov. Sarah Huckabee Sanders.

“These big drug middlemen are only attacking Arkansas in the courts because they’re worried other states will join Governor Sanders in fighting for patient access and affordable prescriptions,” Reuters reported, quoting Sam Dubke, a spokesperson for Sanders.

Named Act 624, the legislation set to take effect on Jan. 1, 2026, bans PBMs from receiving or holding permits to dispense prescription medications and revokes existing permits for PBM-affiliated pharmacies unless they change their business structure.

In addition to CVS Health (NYSE:CVS) and Cigna (NYSE:CI), UnitedHealth Group (UNH) and Elevance Health (ELV) operate PBMs.

data.title: Marvell Technology slides as Wall Street weighs in on results, guidance
data.description:
Marvell Technology (NASDAQ:MRVL) was in focus on Friday after the specialty semiconductor company's first-quarter results and guidance showed its custom AI business is on track, even if more confidence is needed.

Shares fell 3% in premarket trading, while competitors Broadcom (AVGO) and Nvidia (NVDA) were also slightly lower.

Bank of America analyst Vivek Arya, who has a Buy rating and $72 price target on Marvell, said the company's affirmation of its pipeline (coming at the June 17 analyst event) should improve confidence. However, “limited” earnings revisions are likely to keep the stock “in check” in the near-term, as the deal with Microsoft (MSFT) to produce multiple generations of its chips does not start until 2026. 

“We expect mgmt to share more details at its upcoming June-17 AI Investor Event, alongside updated TAM/share outlook across the entire AI portfolio (custom silicon, optics, storage/switching/NIC products),” Arya wrote in a note to clients.

Needham analyst N. Quinn Bolton also offered somewhat tepid enthusiasm, as he lowered his price target to $85 from $100, citing multiple compression in the group. However, he kept Buy rating, as it looks like Marvell has worked through the “noise” and the multiple ramp of its application specific integrated circuit business is on track, via the Microsoft and Amazon (AMZN) deals.

“Management addressed concerns over the next-gen program, stating MRVL is the incumbent, and the company is deeply engaged on the next generation XPU with AMZN,” Bolton wrote in a note to clients. “Regarding the next generation, it has secured 3nm wafer and packaging capacity, and expects to begin production in CY26. Further, it is working with AMZN to support the XPU generation after that. MRVL continues to expect custom XPU revenue at this customer to grow Y/Y in FY26, FY27, and beyond.”

Morgan Stanley analyst Joseph Moore said he had overestimated prices, volumes, or perhaps both for Marvell's custom business with Amazon and its Trainium processors. However, Marvell's other businesses are tracking well and management is still confident in its custom AI business going forward. 

“CES optimism from [management] has not panned out – but growth should be fine from here,” Moore wrote in a note to clients. “Not our favorite stock in AI winners camp, but they do belong in that camp.”

data.title: Silver Storm Mining further upsizes private placement of units
data.description:
Mineral resource exploration company Silver Storm Mining (TSXV:SVRS:CA) announced on Friday an additional increase to the size of its brokered private placement to up to C$10.01M.

The company had said yesterday that the size of its private placement was increased to up to C$8.01M from up to C$6.01M.

The latest upsized offering is comprised of up to 77M units, priced at $0.13 per unit.

The company has granted the agents an option to sell up to an additional 15.4M units at the offering price to raise an additional up to C$2.00M in gross proceeds.

Each unit consists of one share and one share purchase warrant. Each warrant entitles the holder to purchase one share at C$0.20.

The increase comes after Eric Sprott, through 2176423 Ontario Ltd. and current significant shareholder of the company, indicated an intention to participate in the offering.

The net proceeds are intended to be used to commence rehabilitation work of the La Parrilla processing facility, order long lead items required to restart La Parrilla, fund ongoing operations for the next twelve months, and for general corporate and working capital purposes.

data.title: Dividend Roundup: Nvidia, Merck, Lockheed Martin, Meta Platforms, and more

Additionally, looking towards next week, industry players such as Lockheed Martin (NYSE:LMT) and Halliburton (HAL) will see the ex-dividend dates for their upcoming dividend payments.

See a breakdown below of some of the highlights of this week, along with some upcoming activity on the dividend front.

data.title: Germany’s consumer price inflation unchanged at 2.1% in May
data.description:
• Germany’s consumer price inflation remained unchanged at 2.1% in May 2025, in line with April’s figure and marking the lowest rate since October 2024, according to a preliminary estimate. 

  • Core inflation, which excludes volatile energy and food prices, inched down to 2.8% from 2.9% the previous month.

  • On a monthly basis, consumer prices rose by 0.1%, slowing from a 0.4% increase in April.

  • ETFs: (NYSEARCA:EWG), (NYSE:GF), (NASDAQ:FGM), (NASDAQ:DAX), (NYSEARCA:FLGR) (DAX:IND)  
  • Currency: (EUR:USD)

data.title: Nexxen announces transition to reduced $50 million revolving credit facility
data.description:
• Nexxen International (NASDAQ:NEXN) announced on Friday that it amended the revolving credit facility to, among other things, reduce the committed facility size from $90 million to $50 million and extend the maturity to September 2027.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: UP Fintech Q1 earnings benefit from strong trading, asset inflows
data.description:
UP Fintech Holding (NASDAQ:TIGR) American Depositary Shares jumped 4.4% in Friday premarket trading after the online brokerage saw strong trading volume in Q1 2025, added new customers, and saw net asset inflows of $3.4B. 

That added up to record profits for the company that operates as Tiger Brokers.

Trading volume jumped to $217.5M from $198.0M in Q4 2024 and $85.4M in Q1 2024. Number of customer accounts reached 2.53M, up 3.2% Q/Q and 12% Y/Y. The number of customers with deposits was 1.15M, up 12% Q/Q and 24% Y/Y.

"Benefiting from our brand strength and continued investment in R&D, both our GAAP and non-GAAP net income saw impressive growth," said Chairman and CEO Wu Tianhua. "Net income attributable to ordinary shareholders of UP Fintech was $30.4M, up 8.4% quarter over quarter and 146.7% year over year."

Q1 non-GAAP net income per ADS of $0.198 rose from $0.172 in Q4 2024 and $0.092 in last year's Q1.

Q1 total net revenue of $107.6M edged up from $107.4M in the prior quarter and climbed from $64.2M a year ago.

Commissions grew to $58.3M in Q1 2025 from $56.0M in Q4 and $27.8M in last year's Q1. Interest income of $53.8M slipped from $55.8M in the prior quarter and grew from $43.8M in the year-ago period.

Total operating costs and expenses of $67.1M fell from $73.1M in the previous quarter and increased from $50.8M in Q1 2024.

Conference call at 8:00 AM ET.

data.title: So-Young announces plan to implement ADS ratio change, shares down
data.description:
So-Young International (NASDAQ:SY) plans to change the ratio of its American Depositary Shares (ADSs) to Class A ordinary shares, from the current thirteen ADSs representing ten Class A ordinary shares to a new ratio of one ADS representing fifteen Class A ordinary shares.

So-Young International (NASDAQ:SY) shares down 5% premarket.

For the company's ADS holders, the change in the ADS ratio will result in an effect equivalent to a proportional reverse ADS split.

There will be no change to the company's Class A ordinary shares.

The effect of the ratio change on the ADS trading price on Nasdaq is expected to take place at the open of trading on June 30, 2025.

data.title: Zscaler rises after 'solid' Q3, outlook keep analysts bullish
data.description:
Shares of Zscaler (NASDAQ:ZS) rose about 4% premarket on Friday after third quarter fiscal 2025 results beat estimates which also drew positive views from analysts.

BTIG reiterated its Buy rating on the cloud security company and raised the price target on the stock to $298 from $252, noting that it was a solid beat and raise despite macro uncertainty.

Analysts led by Gray Powell said that Zscaler "delivered much better than expected FQ3’25 resultsand guided FQ4’25 billings in-line with street forecasts, effectively passing through the Q3 beat to the full year."

The analysts added that over the last 10 days, they have seen a good amount of noise in the three most recent cybersecurity company reports — Palo Alto Networks (PANW) Okta (OKTA) and SentinelOne (S). Specifically, these companies highlighted some form of a pause in deal activity in April or more conservative forward assumptions around a weak macro environment. Thus, the analysts added that, it was particularly good to see  Zscaler post "clean results."

"Overall, we thought it was a very good print," said Powell and his team. The analysts added that while there are more Security Service Edge, or SSE, offerings gaining traction in the low to mid-market, they continue to see no changes in the competitive environment for large enterprise engagements. In addition, the analysts see proof of continued platform momentum. Disclosures around Zero Trust Everywhere and branch office firewall replacement were encouraging, the analysts noted.

BofA Securities maintained its Buy rating and increased the price target to $285 from $240.

Analysts led by Tal Liani said that Zscaler’s results were strong, with billings/revenue up 25%/23% year-over-year, above Street's 21%/21%, respectively, driven by increasing sales productivity, new products, and billings growth. Margins contracted slightly, however, it relates to the timing of newproduct launches versus a fundamental shift in the business model.

The analysts added that fourth quarter revenue guidance of $706M was slightly softer than the Street’s $711M, which they attribute to conservativism amid the ongoing macro uncertainty.

Liani and his team said they focus instead on the healthy underlying trends supported by strong secular tailwinds and new technology offerings.

Wedbush reaffimed its Outperform rating and raised the price target to $300 from $240.

Analysts led by Daniel Ives said that  Zscaler reported its fiscal third quarter results which featured beats/raised guidance across the board including strong billings beat as the company continues to see strong demand for its zero trust and AI security solutions which will be viewed positively by the Street in this environment.

Ives and his team noted that the company announced it hired Kevin Rubin as the new CFO who will be replacing Remo Canessa which they believe will be a seamless transition at the C-level.

Wells Fargo kept its Equal Weight rating but raised the price target to $260 from $210 noting that the company reported solid third quarter results as it pivots towards a platform.

Analysts led by Andrew Nowinski said Zscaler reported "strong 3Q25 results, solidly above the high-end of guidance, highlighted by a significant billings beat, though management reaffirmed plans to shift away from billings to ARR [annual recurring revenue] in FY26."

The analysts added that the company continues to shift toward a platform approach and now offers a consumption-based pricing model (Z-Flex), which increases the contract duration and provides a significant uplift to ARR and average revenue per user, or ARPU.

Related stocks: Datadog (DDOG) dipped nearly 1%, while Palo Alto Networks (PANW), Okta (OKTA), SentinelOne (S), and Cloudflare (NET) were largely flat but in the red premarket on Friday. Fortinet (FTNT) and CrowdStrike (CRWD) were also largely flat but in the green.
